summary: The session focused on the parameters used in Midjourney for generating images, specifically aspect ratios, chaos values, and seed numbers. The user requested information to be organized into tables for easier readability.
facts: The default aspect ratio in Midjourney is 1:1.
The user requested information to be arranged in a table format for clarity.
The --aspect parameter changes the aspect ratio of generated images in Midjourney.
The --chaos parameter influences the variability of initial image grids, accepting values from 0 to 100.
The --seed parameter allows users to specify a seed number for generating images, accepting whole numbers from 0 to 4294967295.
Using the same seed number and prompt in Midjourney Model Versions 4 and niji will produce identical images.
The --sameseed parameter creates a single large random noise field applied across all images in the initial grid.
Jobs run with random seeds in Midjourney can produce a wide variety of options each time a prompt is used.
The user was provided with a table summarizing the "Quality" parameter settings in Midjourney.
The user was shown how to find a Job's seed number in Discord by reacting with an envelope emoji.
The user was informed that early Midjourney Model Versions are non-deterministic, resulting in similar but not identical images.
The user was instructed on how to change seed numbers by adding --seed or --sameseed to the end of their prompt.
